title: Finding the core vs flexible microbiome
author: Bonnie Hurwitz
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.d2y8fv

steps.title: Locate obese/lean data files
steps.instruction: Look for the obese lean data files in the community data directory: /iplant/home/shared/imicrobe/class/obese_lean SRR029687 - lean T1 SRR029692 - lean T2 SRR029686 -obese T19 SRR029688 - obese T20 You will use the fasta files in this directory for the next step.

steps.title: Run uproc on fasta files
steps.instruction: Click on the Apps button, navigate to experimental->imicrobe->uproc Click on uproc Click on "Required Args" Use the Browse function to navigate to " /iplant/home/shared/imicrobe/class/obese_lean/SRR029687.fasta" under "Sequence file" Run uproc with default parameters on the fasta file Repeat for all of the other fasta files in the obese lean dataset

steps.title: Login to HPC and get data (optional sra toolkit workaround)
steps.instruction: Login to the HPC Some people are having issues with running the sra toolkit on the hpc. You can download these files from iPlant too: % module load irods % icd /iplant/home/shared/imicrobe/class % iget -r obese_lean

steps.title: Load irods and initialize (if first time)
steps.instruction: Load irods on the hpc % module load irods If this is the first time you have used irods, you will need to run: % iinit use the configurations: host-> data.iplantcollaborative.org; port -> 1247; zone -> iplant; user-> your iplant user; login -> your iplant login

steps.title: Transfer and rename uproc output files
steps.instruction: Copy your files from the uproc runs to iPlant and rename them by the file_name.uproc % mkdir obese_lean % cd obese_lean % icd /iplant/home/your_user/analyses/uproc_SRR029687-your-analysis % iget uproc-out % mv uproc-out SRR029687.uproc Repeat for each fasta file

steps.title: Write program to find "core" KEGG IDs
steps.instruction: Write a program that finds all kegg ids that are "core" to each file (in T1, T2, T19, and T20) and have at least 50 read matches. The uproc output files have the following: <kegg_id>,<read count> "core" means that the kegg id is present in each of the uproc files Program name: get_core.pl Output: should be a list of kegg ids

steps.title: Write program to find "variable" KEGG IDs
steps.instruction: Write a program that finds all kegg ids that are "variable" in the files (in T1, T2, T19, and T20) and have at least 50 read matches. The uproc output files have the following: <kegg_id>,<read count> "variable" means that the kegg id is present in 1-3 uproc files, but not all 4. Program name: get_variable.pl Output: should be a list of kegg ids

steps.title: Combine core and variable programs
steps.instruction: Combine the two programs into a single program: get_core_and_variable.pl

steps.title: Submit scripts and results to GitHub
steps.instruction: Submit your scripts and results to github under the folder abe487/obese_lean

steps.title: Discuss "core" and "variable" concepts
steps.instruction: Write up a discussion in the forum on what you think "core" and "variable" means based on the article. What are we trying to find out when we do this?
